result:
That's the standard auto-generated Next.js tsconfig â€” fine to keep. All changes verified working end-to-end. Here's a summary:

## Diagnosis

This site pivoted from **InboxZap** (email-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant) â€” a completely different product. That pivot left the codebase in a broken, self-undermining state:

- **`app/layout.tsx`** still shipped the old title/description/OG tags (`"InboxZap â€” Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"`, `og-inboxzap.png`) on every page, mismatched against the actual Lumina content â€” a likely cause of the homepage's weak 3% CTR despite decent position (4.5).
- **All 12 existing blog posts** target legacy InboxZap keywords (bulk-delete emails, Gmail storage, unsubscribe, inbox zero) â€” ~13,000 impressions combined but only 7 clicks total, because searchers have zero interest in a meeting assistant.
- **`app/blog/page.tsx`** was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist (404) â€” the blog index was a permanent loading spinner with no real links, for users or crawlers.
- **`app/sitemap.ts`** explicitly excluded all blog posts (an unresolved TODO).
- **`/faq`** 301-redirects to `/#faq`, but no `#faq` anchor existed anywhere â€” a dead-end sitewide nav link.
- The old brand name **"InboxZap"** had real remaining equity (its own branded query still ranks #4, 14 clicks) but was mentioned nowhere post-pivot, risking bounces from confused branded searches.

## What I changed
1. **`app/layout.tsx`** â€” corrected title/description/OG/Twitter to Lumina, added `metadataBase`, and added an `Organization` JSON-LD with `alternateName: "InboxZap"` plus a footer mention, so legacy brand search equity transfers instead of being lost.
2. **`lib/posts.js`** â€” new data module distinguishing the 12 legacy (off-topic) slugs from active posts.
3. **`app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`** (new) + **`app/blog/page.tsx`** (rewritten) â€” real server-rendered blog index and post pages, each with proper metadata + `Article` JSON-LD.
4. **3 new blog posts** targeting actual Lumina buyer-intent keywords: meeting minutes templates, AI notetaker comparison, and action-item tracking.
5. **`app/sitemap.ts`** â€” now includes all active blog posts.
6. **`app/page.tsx`** â€” added a real FAQ section (`id="faq"`) with `FAQPage` JSON-LD, including a direct "Is Lumina the same as InboxZap?" answer.
7. **`next.config.js`** â€” 301-redirects all 12 legacy posts to `/blog` (content pruning after the pivot, consolidating equity instead of leaving dead-weight off-topic content indexed).

Verified via `next build` (compiles clean, all pages/posts generate) and a local `next start` smoke test (redirects, FAQ anchor, and new posts all resolve correctly).

**Not done, flagged for you:** the actual `/features`, `/pricing` pages and `og-lumina.png` image referenced throughout aren't in this repo â€” I didn't fabricate them since I have no source of truth for their real content/assets.
